title: dactylorhiza elata subvar. stephensonii
intro: Dactylorhiza elata subvar. stephensonii, also known as Stephenson's marsh orchid, is a subvariety of the Dactylorhiza elata plant species, belonging to the orchid family (Orchidaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Stephenson's marsh orchid are said to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and possessing antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
